verdict_reasoning: HSPD1/HSP60 is canonically a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin with no TM domain or GPI anchor of its own. However, multiple independent lines of evidence document its presence at the plasma membrane outer face: (1) it is explicitly listed as occurring at the 'plasma-cell membrane' and extracellular space in tumor and some normal cells; (2) tumor cells actively secrete/traffic it to the cell surface and into exosomes via non-classical secretion; (3) it functions as a surface signaling/immune-regulatory molecule in cancer contexts. Surface display is cell-state-induced (oncogenic transformation, stress) and not the baseline localization, qualifying as contextual/cell_state_induced rather than yes.
